The inflation rates for the years 2004 and 2005 are expected to be 8% each. The expected price of sugar on January 1, 2006 would be :

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q3 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following question is based on the diagram given below:
1. The rectangle represents government employees.
2. The triangle represents urban people.
3. The circle represents graduates.
4. The square represents clerks.
Which of the above statements is true?

 
Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
The above cases may be considered as under:
For statement (a) to be true, the rectangle should lie inside the square. This is not true. Hence, (a) is false.
For statement (b) to be true, there should be a region common to the rectangle, circle and the
square. Such a region is 6. Hence, (b) is true.
Further, for statement (c) to be true, the rectangle should lie inside the circle. So, (c) is false.
For statement (d) to be true, square should lie wholly inside the rectangle, with no region common
to the circle. This is not true. So, (d) is false.

What is the ratio of sum of circumferences of all the circles to the sum of perimeters of all the squares?

(d)

Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and answer the following question.
Child psychology is certainly not a strong point with most Indian schools; why else would they inflict a double trauma on a student performing badly in the pre-boards by banning her from taking the board exams. Often with fatal results as evidenced by reports of student suicides in the run-up to the boards. Now the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has stepped in and put the brakes on this discriminatory practice, ruling that no student can be barred from the Boards without prior clearance from the CBSE. This is good news for parents and students, many of whom have had to live with the threat of performance-linked debarment. While the school’s logic is that in order to attract talented students, they need to maintain their performance records at high levels. Chances are that a student faring poorly at the pre-boards will replicate at the boards is faulty. Chances are that the student will be spurred to work doubly hard. On the other hand, the threat of debarment, will almost certainly impact adversely on his/her performance. Of course, linking pre-boards to the boards is only one of the problems with our school system.
Which is the good news for parents, according to the passage?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
From the above passage, it is apparent that the good news for parents is that no students can be
barred from the Boards without prior clearance from the CBSE.

NAT

Solution

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Suppose In a vertical microprogrammed control unit, additional hardware such as decoders is needed to generate control signals from microinstructions.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• In vertical microprogramming, each microinstruction is encoded, meaning it contains fewer
bits than the total number of control signals.
• To produce all the required control signals, decoders or other hardware logic are used to
expand these encoded bits.
• This reduces microinstruction size but requires additional decoding hardware.

Q29 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, A, C
Solution

(b)
• A prime attribute of a relation schema R is an attribute that appears in some candidate key of R. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• A relation R is in 3NF, if every non-prime attribute of R, is fully functionally dependent on
some key, because then there will be no transitive dependency. Option (b) is correct.
• Many to one relation can be represented the relation between non-weak entity set. So, option
(c) is incorrect.

Q33 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Spin locks involve busy waiting, so the CPU cycles are consumed while the process waits.
Option (a) is correct.
• A waiting process cannot continue executing other instructions; it is blocked until the mutex is
released. Option (b) is incorrect.
• Priority inversion can occur if a high-priority process spins while waiting for a low-priority
process holding the lock. Option (c) is correct.
• Acquiring the same mutex twice by the same process causes deadlock, unless the mutex is
explicitly recursive. Option (d) is incorrect.

So, the penalty of memory reference instructions in the Pipeline P is 3 cycles and the penalty in the pipeline Q is 5 cycles .If 40% of the instructions are memory

Q39 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• Write-back does not inherently break coherence - coherence must be provided by protocol
(MESI/MOESI, snooping or directory). Inclusion is not guaranteed by write-back. Option (a)
is incorrect.
• Increasing associativity reduces conflict (and related interference) misses because more lines
can reside in the same set. Option (b) is correct.
• Compulsory misses (first-time references) cannot be removed by larger cache or higher
associativity - only prefetching or changing program behavior can avoid them. Option (c) is correct.
• Write-through updates memory on every write, causing more memory traffic than writeback
(which buffers writes and writes back on eviction). Option (d) is correct.

Q46 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a demand paging system, it takes 900 ns to service a page fault if empty page is available or replaced page is not modified. 850 ns time is taken if the replaced page is modified. It is given that main memory has access time of 150 ns. If page to be replaced is modified 60% of time and page fault rate is 15%. Find the average memory access time ________. (in nano seconds) 

NAT
Correct answer: 258
Solution

258
Effective memory access time = (1 – P) × Memory access time + P(Non-modified %
× Page fault service time + Modified % × Page fault service time
with page modified)
= 0.85 × 150 + 0.15 [0.4 × 900 + 0.6 × 850]
= 127.5 + 130.5 = 258

Q60 MCQ Not checked

Suppliers (Sid, Sname, Address)
Parts (Pid, Pname, Color)
Catalog (SidPid, Cost)
What does the following relations algebra query produce?

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Given query retrieve the names of supplier who provide red parts that cost less than 100 dollars.
It first filters red parts and catalog with cost less 100 then join these result to find matching
suppliers IDs and finally uses these IDs to get the corresponding supplier names from suppliers
table.

Q61 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider two concurrent process P and Q with shared variable a and b, two binary semaphore A and B is used which is initialized to 1.

 
What is the value of X1, X2, X3, X4 respectively to satisfy mutual exclusion, progress and bounded waiting?
Correct answer: C, A, B
Solution

(c)
(a) Wait (B), wait (A), wait (A), wait (B).
If X1 is executed in process P and then process is preempted and X3 is executed there is deadlock condition.
(b) Wait (A), wait (B), wait (B), wait (A)
There also deadlock may occurs.
(c) Wait (A), wait (B), wait (A), wait (B)
This is the correct implementation.
